name: American Consumer Credit Counseling, Inc.
description_short: American Consumer Credit Counseling, Inc. (ACCC) is a nonprofit credit counseling agency offering free debt management programs, credit counseling, and bankruptcy education to help consumers reduce debt and achieve financial stability.
description_long:
American Consumer Credit Counseling, Inc. (ACCC) is a nationally recognized nonprofit credit counseling agency serving individuals and families struggling with debt and financial stress. Operating as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, ACCC's primary obligation is to consumers rather than shareholders. The organization is accredited by the Council on Accreditation (COA) and is a member of the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC).

ACCC's core services include comprehensive credit counseling, debt management programs (DMPs), and bankruptcy education. Their debt management program helps clients consolidate debts, negotiate lower interest rates, and reduce monthly payments through direct creditor negotiations. The agency offers pre-bankruptcy counseling for those exploring bankruptcy protection and post-bankruptcy counseling for those recovering from it.

The organization provides free initial consultations to assess your financial situation and explore available options. Clients receive personalized support through a dedicated counselor and can access program details via secure client portal. ACCC focuses on helping consumers regain financial control and achieve long-term debt freedom through education and structured debt management strategies.

All services are provided at no cost to eligible consumers, making ACCC accessible to those with limited financial resources. The nonprofit structure ensures revenue is reinvested into consumer services rather than shareholder profits. ACCC explicitly does not loan money and does not engage in predatory lending. The organization serves clients nationwide.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Nonprofit 501(c)(3) structure aligns incentives with the consumer, not profit generation
Accredited by the Council on Accreditation (COA) and NFCC member — meaningful third-party oversight
HUD-approved housing counseling adds credibility and breadth beyond basic credit services
Reported free initial credit counseling session lowers the barrier to getting help
Exceptionally high customer satisfaction ratings across a large review base (5.0 from 1,400+ reviews)
Nationwide service availability via phone and online, not limited to local walk-in clients
cons: Debt Management Plans require closing enrolled credit accounts, which can negatively impact credit utilization and score in the short term
DMPs reduce interest rates but do not reduce principal — total debt owed remains the same
Creditor participation is not guaranteed; results depend on which lenders agree to ACCC's proposed terms
Multi-year repayment timelines (typically 3–5 years) require sustained financial discipline that not all clients can maintain
Services are primarily suited to unsecured debt (credit cards, personal loans) — less useful for secured debt like mortgages or auto loans
best_for: Consumers in Atlanta, Georgia looking for credit repair services
People who prefer working with a local credit repair provider
Individuals with negative items on their credit reports
People preparing for major purchases like home or car buying
diagnosis: ACCC is best suited for consumers carrying high-interest unsecured debt who want a structured, nonprofit-guided repayment path and prefer a legitimate counseling approach over debt settlement. It is less appropriate for those seeking immediate debt reduction, those with primarily secured debt, or anyone unwilling to close credit accounts and commit to a multi-year plan.
services: Debt Management Plan (DMP) enrollment and creditor negotiation
Bankruptcy counseling and pre-discharge education certificates
Financial literacy workshops and educational resources
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 260 Peachtree St NW Suite 2200, Atlanta, GA 30303
